materialChanges: Added precise claims for geometric scaling, right-triangle geometry, and right-triangle ratios; corrected every claim-to-scene and learner-visible source-note mapping; added one NASA mathematics reference; and expanded the existing DLMF record to its exact inverse-function and principal-value section.
Explained dimensional scale powers and the unit-circle identity, then added real principal ranges, a quadrant-aware direction procedure, an axis boundary, and a non-first-quadrant example.

Rebuilt the inverse-versus-reciprocal misconception as a commit-shortcut-disconfirmation-repair-recheck sequence, labeled the reciprocal example in radians, added a worked-example self-explanation check, and protected practice retrieval before feedback.

Replaced the one-vector transfer with a rotated-rectangle clearance model requiring two perpendicular projections and revised the mastery items to assess that model plus a quadrant-II inverse-trigonometry case with correct rounding.

Updated glossary, scene metadata, agent-assistance disclosure, and attribution only where required by the revised teaching and provenance record; lesson identity, version, status, risk tier, outcomes, and publication state remain unchanged.

reviewDispositions.rationale: The misconception scene now begins with a learner commitment, names the tempting shortcut, disproves it with a unit-labeled numerical contrast, states the repair, and ends with a new-case recheck before feedback.

The transfer and corresponding mastery task now require modeling the vertical extent of a rotated rectangle by combining projections from two perpendicular edges, making a clearance decision, and applying distinct linear and area scale factors; this requires model selection beyond the worked one-vector decomposition.
The concept scene now derives the square and cube scale powers by multiplying two and three scaled length dimensions, aligning the explanation with retrieval and assessment.
A second worked-example check now asks why tangent matches the two known legs and how positive coordinate signs constrain the direction to quadrant I, with reasoning-rich feedback.
The practice scene now instructs learners to cover or skip feedback, commit a complete answer first, and treats every answer line as feedback to inspect only after commitment.
The unit-circle section now states x squared plus y squared equals one and explicitly substitutes x equals cosine and y equals sine to obtain the Pythagorean identity.

The claim ledger now separates geometric scaling, right-triangle geometry, and right-triangle ratios from analytic trigonometric relations; their exact scene mappings and every affected source note now resolve to the added NASA mathematics reference.

The existing DLMF record now identifies both section 4.14 and exact section 4.23(i)-(ii) for inverse functions and principal values, while the NASA record directly supports right-triangle ratios, Pythagorean geometry, area/volume dimensions, and Cartesian-polar coordinate relations.

The concept scene now teaches real principal ranges, sign-based quadrant selection, all four quadrant mappings, and the x-equals-zero boundary; a quadrant-II assessment requires converting a reference angle to 118.07 degrees, and both inverse and quadrant claims now map to the concept and synthesis evidence.
The truncated 61.92-degree response is no longer accepted; the revised inverse item requires and accepts the correctly rounded 118.07-degree quadrant-II direction.
The reciprocal comparison now specifies a 0.5-radian angle input and gives the independently checkable value of approximately 2.0858, distinct from the arcsine angle output.

evidenceChecked: All seven scenes retain one level-one heading, meaningful subordinate headings, manifest order as reading order, and text-first instructions that reflow without fixed coordinates or viewport assumptions.

Every geometric relation is available through named sides, coordinates, perpendicular relationships, equations, and prose; no diagram, image, color, sound, motion, hover, drag, pointer, or timed operation carries unique information.

Practice, misconception repair, transfer, and assessment provide explicit commit-before-feedback or after-attempt routes; the assessment remains untimed and retakeable with textual prompts, answer reasoning, common errors, rubrics, and remediation links.

Math uses dollar-delimited constrained TeX with prose interpretations; scene Markdown contains no raw HTML, entities, remote embeds, Markdown images, scripts, event handlers, unsafe links, executable code, or active assets.
Claim-to-scene, claim-to-source, and same-scene learner-visible source-note coverage was recomputed for all eight claims and all seven scenes with no missing or extra identifiers.

All three references have allowed source types, single ISO dates, bounded bibliographic fields, factual-reference uses, facts-only-original-expression rights bases, rights evidence, and dated agent-access records.

The new NASA source is used only for standard mathematical facts and relations; no NASA wording, examples, images, page organization, marks, or third-party material is reused. The NIST sources likewise support facts without reused expression.
The pack declares no third-party assets or datasets and contains no personal, patient, learner, credential, secret, clinical, or engineering-certification data or advice.

ATTRIBUTION.md and lesson metadata retain the accountable author and candidate agent disclosure; the two reviews retain their own provenance; this adjudication discloses the fresh finalizer run and instruction digest.
limitations: This was a static source audit; no browser renderer, screen reader, keyboard session, 200-percent zoom/reflow session, print rendering, or unavailable non-web export adapter was exercised.

The run boundary prohibited reading site/ schemas and policy files and prohibited npm, so repository JSON-Schema validation, strict source preflight, and the canonical npm verification gate were not run; only pack-local JSON, mapping, calculation, Markdown-safety, and field-constraint checks were completed.

GitHub COMMENTED transport eligibility was supplied by the accountable operator and was not independently queried from GitHub in this run; COMMENTED was treated as transport rather than a dismissal as instructed.
Agent/provider/model provenance and provider diversity are self-reported declarations by the accountable operator and were checked only for internal distinctness, not independently verified.

rationale: The frozen candidate content remained byte-identical through both eligible advisory reviews. The standard review-input gate is satisfied by one academic and one learning-design request-changes artifact against the exact candidate, across two distinct declared runs and two distinct declared providers; this is disclosed provenance, not verified independence. This fresh finalizer incorporated all eleven indexed findings in one coherent revision, recalculated the changed answers, repaired the evidence chain, and completed the source-level accessibility-and-rights audit. Pack-local checks found valid JSON, complete claim/source-note coverage, coherent objective/item/rubric links, string-only acceptable responses, valid reference types/dates/field bounds, one level-one heading per scene, and no raw HTML, unsafe TeX, or active content. No substantive blocker or unincorporated dissent remains. Merge is therefore the appropriate content decision, subject to replacing the required final-commit placeholder and passing the operator-reserved schema, source-preflight, repository, and GitHub provenance checks before any actual merge.
dissent: The learning-design reviewer requested changes on six findings in the original candidate; all six are incorporated in the final revision, so no learning-design blocker remains unresolved.
The academic reviewer requested changes on five findings in the original candidate; all five are incorporated in the final revision, so no academic blocker remains unresolved.
conditions: After committing the final content and both unchanged review artifacts, replace PENDING-FINAL-COMMIT with that exact 40-character commit SHA; do not change teaching content after that commit.
Run the canonical schema, strict source-preflight, repository validation, and GitHub provenance gates outside this prohibited-no-npm run, and do not merge if any gate fails.
After finalization, the maintainer may make only the separately authorized publication-state transition for lesson status, source confidence, and retained claim review-status fields.

adjudicator.displayName: VSBDev — accountable fresh finalizer and adjudicator
